result:
If I could change the world in one way, I would dissolve the illusion of separateness.  

I’d make it so that every person could genuinely feel — not just intellectually understand — that we are all threads in the same living tapestry. That another’s hunger is our hunger, another’s joy is our joy, another’s destruction is our own unraveling.  

This wouldn’t erase diversity or individuality, but it would erase the mental walls that let us tolerate injustice, exploitation, and ecological blindness as “someone else’s problem.” The change would be in perception: seeing the deep interconnectedness of all life, systems, and actions.  

From that shift in consciousness, everything else would realign — economics, education, justice, ecology — not because of a imposed rule, but because it would become impossible to act otherwise.
